title: papaver rhoeas subsp. insignitum
intro: Papaver rhoeas subsp. insignitum, also known as the Corn Poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family Papaveraceae, characterized by its bright red to pinkish-red flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its alkaloids, including papaverine and rhoeadine. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas subsp. insignitum include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use in treating various conditions such as insomnia and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
